title: Parallax Write Instruction (m-unit-work)
description: The canonical, language-neutral write-instruction vocabulary a unit of work buffers and flushes (m-unit-work) — the write-side analogue of `operation.schema.json`. A write from any frontend lowers into exactly one instruction of one of two shapes: a `keyed` instruction (`mutation` + `entity` + `rows`, the flat attribute-named write input) or a `predicate` instruction (`mutation` + `target` {entity, predicate} + `assignments`, the set-based write). Embedded predicates are canonical `m-op-algebra` nodes, legal here because `m-unit-work` already depends on `m-op-algebra`; this schema treats a predicate as an opaque object and defers its validation to `operation.schema.json`, exactly as `compatibility-case.schema.json` does for a `writeTarget.predicate`. The instant surface is AXIS-EXPLICIT: business bounds are named uniformly `businessFrom` / `businessTo` (never the corpus's overloaded `at` or the split `businessAt` / `until` authoring aliases). The PROCESSING instant is deliberately NOT an instruction field — it is harness / Clock-Strategy supplied context bound at flush (ADR 0010), so no caller-facing instruction shape can smuggle one in. The framework-owned transaction OBSERVATION (optimistic version / observed `in_z`) is likewise not an instruction field; it is attached per materialized row at flush. `compatibility-case.schema.json` keeps the corpus's `at` / `businessAt` / `until` spellings as authoring aliases of these canonical names (the corpus-wide re-authoring to the canonical spellings is deferred). SERDE ROUND-TRIP CONTRACT: a conforming implementation MUST round-trip every instruction through this canonical form losslessly — `serialize(deserialize(x)) == x` (JSON and YAML) — the write-side of the `m-op-algebra` serde contract.

keyedWriteInstruction.description: A keyed write: a mutation on one entity carrying the flat attribute-named neutral write input (`rows`). The MVP non-temporal / audit-only surface is `insert` / `update` / `delete` / `terminate`; the bounded full-bitemporal rectangle split adds `insertUntil` / `updateUntil` / `terminateUntil`. A bounded `*Until` mutation carries an explicit `businessTo`; a plain (unbounded) temporal mutation carries only `businessFrom` (its window runs `[businessFrom, infinity)`). Deferred extension verbs (`cascadeDelete`, business-only, the MAY-tier recovery / increment mutations) are added to `mutation` when their slices claim them, without reshaping the instruction.

rows.description: The neutral write input: one or more flat attribute-named rows the mutation operates on (`$defs/writeRow`). A generating implementation classifies each field by its metamodel role and derives the emitted column list and ORDER from `columnOrder(entity)`.

businessFrom.description: The BUSINESS-axis lower bound of the mutation's valid-time window (`from_z`). Present on a temporal (audit-only / business / bitemporal) mutation; the corpus authoring aliases are `at` on a bitemporal `insertUntil` step and `businessAt` on a business-only step.

businessTo.description: The BUSINESS-axis exclusive upper bound of a bounded (`*Until`) mutation's valid-time window (`thru_z`). Present ONLY on `insertUntil` / `updateUntil` / `terminateUntil`; the corpus authoring alias is `until`. A plain (unbounded) temporal mutation omits it — its window runs `[businessFrom, infinity)`.

allOf.description: A bounded `*Until` mutation MUST carry its business window's exclusive upper bound `businessTo`; a plain (unbounded) mutation MUST NOT.

predicateWriteInstruction.description: A predicate-selected (set-based) write: a mutation on every row of `target.entity` matching `target.predicate`, with `assignments` on the update forms. This is the set-based analogue of the keyed instruction; `m-batch-write` / `m-opt-lock` decide whether it lowers readless (unversioned non-temporal) or materializes per-row (versioned / temporal). Assignment list order is DATA order only — descriptor `columnOrder(entity)` determines the emitted SET columns and their binds.

businessFrom.description: The BUSINESS-axis lower bound for a plain or bounded temporal predicate write (`from_z`). The corpus authoring alias is `businessFrom` already (predicate writes were authored axis-explicit from the start, COR-35).

businessTo.description: The BUSINESS-axis exclusive upper bound of a bounded (`*Until`) predicate write's window (`thru_z`). Present ONLY on `updateUntil` / `terminateUntil`; the corpus authoring alias is `until`.

allOf.description: The assignment-bearing update forms (`update` / `updateUntil`) MUST carry `assignments`; `delete` / `terminate` / `terminateUntil` MUST NOT.
A bounded `*Until` predicate write MUST carry its business window's exclusive upper bound `businessTo`; the unbounded forms MUST NOT.

writeTarget.description: The exact entity a predicate-selected write begins from plus its bare `m-op-algebra` predicate. The predicate is validated against `operation.schema.json` and the descriptor-dependent scope / bare-predicate rules separately (it must be a bare write predicate, never a result modifier).

writeAssignment.description: One ordered predicate-write assignment. `attr` is a qualified descriptor attribute or top-level value-object reference and `value` is a neutral literal: a scalar / null for an attribute or the complete object / array document for a value object. Duplicate and unassignable (framework-owned / PK / relationship) references are rejected by model-aware validation.

writeRow.description: A flat attribute-named write row — the neutral write input, same vocabulary as a fixture row. The reserved control key `observedVersion` is named explicitly; every OTHER key is an entity ATTRIBUTE name or a top-level VALUE-OBJECT name (a business/developer name like `id` / `orderedOn` / `address`, never a physical column). A scalar attribute's value is a scalar literal, an explicit `null`, or a one-key DB-computed marker (`{ computed: "maxPlusOne" }` or `{ increment: <n> }`); a value-object field's value is the WHOLE embedded composite document (a JSON object, or a JSON array of objects for a `many` value object), or `null`. A generating implementation classifies each field by its metamodel role and derives the emitted column list and ORDER from `columnOrder(entity)`, binding a value object atomically as one document in its columnOrder position; a marker-shaped value is a DB-computed marker vs a value-object document by MODEL ROLE, not by the value's shape — so the row cannot be `additionalProperties: false`.

observedVersion.description: The optimistic-lock version the unit of work read (m-opt-lock). A reserved control key, NOT an attribute/column: the advance (`observedVersion + 1`) and the `and version = ?` gate are derived, never authored. It rides on the neutral write input but the framework-owned observation itself is a flush-time input, never durable instruction state.

anyOf.description: A value-object document (m-value-object): a value-object-named field binds the WHOLE embedded composite atomically as one document value in its columnOrder position — a JSON object for a `one` value object, a JSON array of objects for a `many` one. The value branches are non-exclusive (`anyOf`): a marker-shaped value-object document validates via both this branch and the computed-marker branch, and the model role decides.

writeComputedMarker.description: A one-key DB-COMPUTED marker in a neutral write row: the attribute's value is derived by the database rather than authored as a literal bind, so the generating implementation emits the strategy's SQL fragment. Applies ONLY to a scalar-attribute column (a value-object column's value is always the literal document even when marker-shaped, disambiguated by metamodel role, not by shape).

computed.description: The pk-gen `max` strategy: the column is emitted as `coalesce(max(col), ?) + ?` — the computation folds into the INSERT's SQL, so a `max`-strategy insert stays statically derivable.

increment.description: A self-referential advance emitted as `col = col + ?` (e.g. a sequence registry's `next_val`); the integer is the amount added.

instant.description: A business-axis instant: an ISO-8601 UTC timestamp string, or the open-bound sentinel `infinity`.

entityName.description: An exact concrete descriptor entity name (the position a write begins from).
